title: Workflow Syntax, Expressions, YAML, and Data Contracts · CI/CD Control Room
summary: A delivery pairing session predicts each automation transition before producing the first working validated workflow data contract for workflow syntax, expressions, yaml, and data contracts. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of validated workflow data contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Author name, on, permissions, concurrency, env, defaults, jobs, and job-level contracts with explicit supported syntax.
Control YAML scalars, booleans, quoting, multiline blocks, reserved-looking keys, indentation, and serialization boundaries.
Reason about expression literals, loose equality, coercion, truthiness, functions, object filters, fromJSON, and toJSON without leaking data.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Workflow Schema Top Level
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop workflow parses YAML but treats false as text, expands a multiline value unsafely, uses an unavailable context, and accepts a misspelled input. Validate syntax, types, timing, shell boundaries, and a changed event fixture.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Workflow Schema Top Level.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for author name, on, permissions, concurrency, env, defaults, jobs, and job-level contracts with explicit supported syntax. steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Workflow Static Validation
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-workflow-static-validation-8sof5k". Tailor its workflow-contract.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-workflow-static-validation-8sof5k
  - competency: ci-workflow-static-validation
    artifact: workflow-contract.yaml
    decision: workflow static validation
    environment: public fork pull request with no repository write access
    fault-injection: rotate a cloud trust condition during deployment
    retained-proof: lock hash cold install test report package contents and output digest
    case-key: 8sof5k
    change: |
      name: verified-ci
      on-events: [pull_request, push]
      permissions: contents-read
      concurrency: revision-scoped
      expression-input: parsed-with-fromJSON
      shell-input: validated-environment
      schema: action-inputs-reviewed
      changed-event-fixture: fork-pull-request
    verify: validate workflow schema expressions context availability action inputs and changed event fixtures
    failure: Reject valid-YAML-only review, implicit coercion, unavailable contexts, or direct untrusted expression interpolation.
    repair: Validate workflow semantics, parse explicit types, move untrusted values through data boundaries, and test changed payloads.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine schema validation, action input review, expression linting, local deterministic inspection, changed-event fixtures, and GitHub-run evidence.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Ci Workflow Static Validation, name the event, revision, actor, permission, runner, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, and recovery boundary, Ci Workflow Static Validation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Workshop Ci Workflow Static Validation, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A workshop workflow parses YAML but treats false as text, expands a multiline value unsafely, uses an unavailable context, and accepts a misspelled input. Validate syntax, types, timing, shell boundaries, and a changed event fixture. Competency probe: Combine schema validation, action input review, expression linting, local deterministic inspection, changed-event fixtures, and GitHub-run evidence.

For Defend the transferred model · Workshop Ci Workflow Static Validation, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A local workflow emulator proves hosted-runner, token, event, and service behavior.
